summary: The Docker build failed because a RUN step that downloads the nydus static tarball with wget returned a 502 Bad Gateway, causing the build step to exit with code 1 and buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: The RUN step executing wget to fetch the nydus release from GitHub failed with 'HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error', causing the command to exit with code 1 and the build to fail.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Shows the wget command failed with a 502 response from the remote server.

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Confirms the failing process was the wget command and that it exited with a non-zero status, causing the solve to fail.
Identifies the Dockerfile and line where the failing RUN wget command is located.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the top-level CI action (buildx bake) failed as a result of the wget/solve failure.

suggested_fix: Retry the workflow (this appears to be a transient HTTP 502). If failures persist, modify the Dockerfile to add retry/backoff to the download (e.g., curl/wget with --retry), use a cached or mirrored copy of the nydus artifact, or vendor the release into your build cache so the build does not depend on a single external host.

summary: A functional test failed when pip attempted to download a package from GitHub and received repeated 502 responses, causing a network-related test failure.
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip due to repeated 502 responses from github.com, causing an OSError and non-zero exit code.

evidence.reason: Shows the failing test was rerun (indicates a flaky/network-involving test).
Identifies the specific test that failed.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: The pip command failed with an OSError caused by repeated 502 responses from GitHub while attempting to download the test package.
The test harness recorded a non-zero exit code from the pip install command, causing the test to fail.
Summary confirming a single failing test among many passing tests, consistent with an isolated/flaky/network issue.

suggested_fix: Rerun the failing job (the failure is caused by transient 502 responses from github.com). If failures recur, avoid depending on GitHub for this test by mocking the network call or hosting the test package on a reliable local test server / fixture so tests do not fail due to external HTTP 502 errors.

summary: One Jest test suite failed: a test in test/config/get.ts emitted an unexpected warning about an environment variable replacement and the test run exited with status 1.
root_cause: A test in test/config/get.ts failed due to an unexpected warning: "[WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}" which is reported from test/config/get.ts (line 32), causing the Jest run to exit with status 1.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that one test suite failed while most others passed.

evidence.quote: [WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}
evidence.reason: The unexpected warning message that likely triggered the failing assertion or indicates missing environment variable substitution.
Stack trace points to the failing test location inside test/config/get.ts at line 32.
Shows the test run exited with a non-zero status, confirming the CI job failed because Jest returned exit code 1.

suggested_fix: Provide the expected environment variable before running tests (e.g. set ENV_VAR_123 in the CI environment) or update the test/config/get.ts test to mock or handle missing environment variables so it does not emit the warning/trigger a failure.
